commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Slack and Telegram channel docs to explain `ackReactionScope`, its default `group-mentions` behavior, and the DM opt-in values.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: current main shows Slack and Telegram providers read `cfg.messages?.ackReactionScope` only, while the PR documents account/channel scope locations.
commands.review_followup: A narrow docs repair can make the PR accurate without touching runtime behavior.

target.title: docs(channels): document ackReactionScope for Slack & Telegram (DM gotcha)
target.body:
## Summary

The Slack and Telegram channel docs cover `ackReaction` (which emoji to send) but never mention `ackReactionScope` (when to actually send it). Because the default scope is `group-mentions`, setting `ackReaction: "eyes"` on a Slack DM-only deployment looks broken: the bot has the `reactions:write` scope, the config validates, but no reaction ever fires. The actual reason — the scope gate explicitly excludes DMs — only shows up in `src/channels/ack-reactions.ts` and `docs/channels/matrix.md`.

This PR brings the Slack and Telegram docs in line with `docs/channels/matrix.md`.

## Changes

- **`docs/channels/slack.md`**
  - Split *Ack reactions* into "Emoji (`ackReaction`)" and "Scope (`ackReactionScope`)".
  - Document scope resolution order (per-account → channel → `messages.ackReactionScope` → default `group-mentions`).
  - Document scope values (`all`, `direct`, `group-all`, `group-mentions`, `off`/`none`).
  - Add a `<Note>` callout flagging that the default does **not** fire in DMs, and that `messages.ackReactionScope` is read at provider startup (gateway restart required to apply changes).
  - Add a short JSON example using `ackReactionScope: "all"`.
- **`docs/channels/telegram.md`** — same structure inside the existing `<Accordion>`.

No behavior changes; docs only.

## How this came up (AI-assisted)

A user configured `channels.slack.ackReaction: "eyes"` on a Slack DM, restarted the gateway, confirmed `reactions:write` was on the bot token, and saw no reaction. Tracing through `dist/ack-reactions-CWvuwWwb.js` → `shouldAckReaction()` showed that with `scope = "group-mentions"` and `isDirect = true`, the function returns `false` before it ever calls the Slack API. Fixing it required setting `messages.ackReactionScope: "all"` (or `"direct"`). The docs gap was the actual root cause; the rest was just config.

## Test plan

- `pnpm exec node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/slack.md docs/channels/telegram.md` → passes.
- `pnpm exec node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check` → clean (full docs corpus).
- `pnpm dlx markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/channels/slack.md docs/channels/telegram.md` → 0 errors.

## Checklist

- [x] Marked as AI-assisted (in the PR description and the commit trailer).
- [x] Human-run verification: reproduced the surprise locally before writing, then applied `ackReactionScope: "all"` and confirmed `:eyes:` reactions started firing on Slack DM inbound messages.
- [ ] No session logs attached (small docs change); happy to share if useful.
target.author: drclaw-iq
